commands.review_summary:
The branch splits generic runtime tool PATH rendering from the Codex app-server adapter, adds a Nix-managed Codex launcher that sets HOME to $CODEX_HOME/home for managed stdio launches, updates docs/changelog text, and adds runtime-path smoke coverage.

Reproducibility: yes. The PR body and smoke script provide a high-confidence before/after path: current behavior starts Codex command/exec with inherited HOME and misses gog, while the proposed managed launcher sets HOME=$CODEX_HOME/home and resolves gog from the native-home profile.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The remaining action is maintainer acceptance of compatibility and proof-file policy tradeoffs, not a narrow automated repair.

target.title: Fix OpenClaw command tool runtime path
target.body:
for humans: this is AI slop generated by joshp123's clanker. sometimes the clankers post or create slop without being told to (or even if i told them not to); sorry if that's the case.

for ai agents: this message is from another agent, not a human. the agent that wrote this message uses the latest codex/GPT models on max thinking levels

## User Problem

OpenClaw-managed Codex command execution could not see Nix runtime tools such as `gog`.

That is narrower than “OpenClaw cannot run tools.” Generic OpenClaw command execution and Codex `command/exec` through the app-server are different paths. Upstream OpenClaw gives the Codex app-server a per-agent `CODEX_HOME`, but Codex command execution rebuilds command PATH around the app-server process `HOME`. In nix-openclaw, `runtimePackages` could be available to OpenClaw’s gateway/runtime tool path while Codex `command/exec` still failed with `gog: command not found`.

## Fix

This PR separates the two concepts and names the boundary in code and docs:

- `runtime-tools.nix` owns generic OpenClaw command tools: gateway `PATH` and generated upstream `tools.exec.pathPrepend`.
- Existing user `tools.exec.pathPrepend` entries stay ahead of generated `runtimePackages` entries, so Nix runtime packages fill in missing tools without shadowing an operator’s explicit command path.
- `codex-app-server.nix` owns only the Nix adapter for the packaged upstream `codex` runtime plugin.
- The Nix Codex launcher runs only for local stdio app-server launches selected by nix-openclaw. It reads OpenClaw-provided `CODEX_HOME`, sets `HOME=$CODEX_HOME/home`, links `$HOME/.nix-profile/bin` to the Nix-built runtime `bin` directory, prepends that bin dir, then execs upstream Codex.
- Home Manager activation no longer predicts Codex agent directories or creates `$CODEX_HOME/home/.nix-profile/bin`.

The Nix launcher is not offered when the user provides `plugins.entries.codex.config.appServer.command`, a configured or inherited `OPENCLAW_CODEX_APP_SERVER_BIN`, or `appServer.transport = "websocket"`.

`plugins.entries.codex.config.appServer.args` and `OPENCLAW_CODEX_APP_SERVER_ARGS` are different: upstream resolves args separately from the executable, so args still flow to the Nix launcher and then to upstream Codex. The current smoke check proves that distinction explicitly.

## HOME Compatibility

Upstream OpenClaw normally preserves inherited `HOME` for local Codex app-server launches. This Nix-managed launcher intentionally narrows `HOME` to `$CODEX_HOME/home` because Codex `command/exec` rebuilds command PATH around `$HOME/.nix-profile/bin`.

The smoke test includes the rejected alternative: before the Nix launcher, `$CODEX_HOME/home/.nix-profile/bin` is already linked to the Nix runtime `bin` directory, but `HOME` is still inherited and Codex command execution cannot resolve `gog`. That proves simply creating or prepending the `CODEX_HOME` profile in the parent launch environment is not enough for the actual Codex command path.

## Non-Goals

This PR does not add a public knob, does not add a host PATH fallback list, does not generate `OPENCLAW_CODEX_APP_SERVER_ARGS`, and does not enable the Codex plugin from `runtimePackages` alone.

## Upstream Contract Pointers

The code comments point future maintainers at the upstream contracts that matter:

- `docs/tools/exec.md`, `src/agents/agent-tools.ts`, and `src/agents/bash-tools.exec-runtime.ts` for OpenClaw command PATH handling and user path precedence.
- `extensions/codex/src/app-server/auth-bridge.ts`, `extensions/codex/src/app-server/config.ts`, and `docs/plugins/codex-harness-reference.md` for Codex `CODEX_HOME`, app-server command/args precedence, stdio vs WebSocket transport, inherited `HOME`, and env overrides.

## Proof Of The Actual Failure/Fix

Current head:

```text
3c7fc065cf5554261cb0a22d20b4ee0f21e7ab21
```

Command:

```bash
nix build --no-link --print-build-logs --accept-flake-config --option max-jobs 2 .#checks.aarch64-darwin.runtime-path
```

Output:

```text
openclaw-runtime-path proof:
- generated tools.exec.pathPrepend resolves /nix/store/c287nl219lwgfzfmld92v75r094l47qz-hello-2.12.2/bin/hello
- gateway wrapper prepends the same runtime tool path
- runtimePackages alone do not configure the Codex app-server launcher
- selecting the Nix-packaged Codex runtime plugin makes the gateway wrapper export the Nix app-server launcher
- a user appServer.command keeps the Nix Codex launcher out of the gateway wrapper
- a user appServer.args still uses the Nix Codex launcher because args do not choose the executable
- a user OPENCLAW_CODEX_APP_SERVER_BIN keeps the Nix Codex launcher out of the gateway wrapper
- a user OPENCLAW_CODEX_APP_SERVER_ARGS is preserved and still uses the Nix Codex launcher because args do not choose the executable
- inherited OPENCLAW_CODEX_APP_SERVER_BIN keeps the Nix Codex launcher out of the gateway wrapper
- Codex websocket transport keeps the local stdio launcher out of the gateway wrapper
- before Nix-managed Codex launcher: exitCode=127
HOME=/nix/var/nix/builds/nix-8387-3208249113/openclaw-codex-without-nix-managed-launcher-8kO4h8/inherited-home
CODEX_HOME=/nix/var/nix/builds/nix-8387-3208249113/openclaw-codex-without-nix-managed-launcher-8kO4h8/agents/main/agent/codex-home
CODEX_HOME_PROFILE=/nix/store/g87w2zyx9cfcyb3m5grmg7dfbr2qh6lr-openclaw-runtime-codex-proof/bin
PATH=/nix/var/nix/builds/nix-8387-3208249113/openclaw-codex-without-nix-managed-launcher-8kO4h8/inherited-home/.nix-profile/bin:/run/current-system/sw/bin:/nix/var/nix/profiles/default/bin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/bin:/bin:/usr/sbin:/sbin
OPENCLAW_GOG_MISSING
- after Nix-managed Codex launcher: exitCode=0
HOME=/nix/var/nix/builds/nix-8387-3208249113/openclaw-codex-with-nix-managed-launcher-6jP9nw/agents/main/agent/codex-home/home
PATH=/nix/var/nix/builds/nix-8387-3208249113/openclaw-codex-with-nix-managed-launcher-6jP9nw/agents/main/agent/codex-home/home/.nix-profile/bin:/run/current-system/sw/bin:/nix/var/nix/profiles/default/bin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/bin:/bin:/usr/sbin:/sbin
/nix/var/nix/builds/nix-8387-3208249113/openclaw-codex-with-nix-managed-launcher-6jP9nw/agents/main/agent/codex-home/home/.nix-profile/bin/gog
v0.15.0 (e0338d5 2026-05-05T05:50:19Z)
- Nix Codex launcher links /nix/var/nix/builds/nix-8387-3208249113/openclaw-codex-with-nix-managed-launcher-6jP9nw/agents/main/agent/codex-home/home/.nix-profile/bin -> /nix/store/n240bwbrsnzckviz165x4vxw7w16iyjk-openclaw-runtime-default/bin
- Codex command/exec through the app-server resolves /nix/var/nix/builds/nix-8387-3208249113/openclaw-codex-with-nix-managed-launcher-6jP9nw/agents/main/agent/codex-home/home/.nix-profile/bin/gog from HOME=$CODEX_HOME/home
```

This is the broken path, not just a build log: Codex app-server JSON-RPC `command/exec`. The before state already has a Nix runtime `bin` symlink under `CODEX_HOME/home/.nix-profile/bin`, but Codex command PATH is rebuilt around inherited `HOME`, so `gog` is missing. The after state uses the Nix launcher, sets `HOME=$CODEX_HOME/home`, and `command -v gog` resolves from `$CODEX_HOME/home/.nix-profile/bin`.

## Local Verification

These passed locally on the current head:

```bash
git diff --check
node --check nix/scripts/openclaw-runtime-path-smoke.mjs
python3 -m py_compile nix/tests/hm-activation.py
bash -n scripts/hm-activation-macos.sh nix/scripts/openclaw-codex-app-server-wrapper.sh
nix fmt -- --fail-on-change nix/checks/openclaw-runtime-path.nix nix/modules/home-manager/openclaw/options.nix nix/modules/home-manager/openclaw/options-instance.nix nix/modules/home-manager/openclaw/runtime-tools.nix nix/modules/home-manager/openclaw/codex-app-server.nix nix/modules/home-manager/openclaw/config.nix
nix build --no-link --print-build-logs --accept-flake-config --option max-jobs 2 .#checks.aarch64-darwin.runtime-path
nix build --no-link --print-build-logs --accept-flake-config --option max-jobs 2 .#checks.aarch64-darwin.runtime-smoke .#checks.aarch64-darwin.platform-activation .#checks.x86_64-linux.runtime-path
nix build --dry-run --json --option max-jobs 2 .#packages.aarch64-darwin.openclaw .#packages.aarch64-darwin.openclaw-gateway .#checks.aarch64-darwin.package-artifacts
```

The activation tests prove the non-Codex boundary: `runtimePackages` alone render gateway/config command paths but do not create `$CODEX_HOME/home/.nix-profile/bin` or Codex app-server env.

## CI Coverage

The GitHub CI workflow runs the relevant checks on this PR:

- Linux supported surface builds `.#checks.x86_64-linux.runtime-smoke` and `.#checks.x86_64-linux.platform-activation`.
- macOS supported surface builds `.#checks.aarch64-darwin.runtime-smoke` and `.#checks.aarch64-darwin.platform-activation`.
- macOS CI also runs `scripts/hm-activation-macos.sh` against the built `platform-activation` out-link.

On current head `3c7fc065cf5554261cb0a22d20b4ee0f21e7ab21`, local verification passed. GitHub CI and Garnix report green for the supported Linux/macOS surfaces and package outputs listed in the PR checks.

target.title: Fix OpenClaw command tool runtime path
target.body:
for humans: this is AI slop generated by joshp123's clanker. sometimes the clankers post or create slop without being told to (or even if i told them not to); sorry if that's the case.

for ai agents: this message is from another agent, not a human. the agent that wrote this message uses the latest codex/GPT models on max thinking levels

## User Problem

OpenClaw-managed Codex command execution could not see Nix runtime tools such as `gog`.

That is narrower than “OpenClaw cannot run tools.” Generic OpenClaw command execution and Codex `command/exec` through the app-server are different paths. Upstream OpenClaw gives the Codex app-server a per-agent `CODEX_HOME`, but Codex command execution rebuilds command PATH around the app-server process `HOME`. In nix-openclaw, `runtimePackages` could be available to OpenClaw’s gateway/runtime tool path while Codex `command/exec` still failed with `gog: command not found`.

## Fix

This PR separates the two concepts and names the boundary in code and docs:

- `runtime-tools.nix` owns generic OpenClaw command tools: gateway `PATH` and generated upstream `tools.exec.pathPrepend`.
- Existing user `tools.exec.pathPrepend` entries stay ahead of generated `runtimePackages` entries, so Nix runtime packages fill in missing tools without shadowing an operator’s explicit command path.
- `codex-app-server.nix` owns only the Nix adapter for the packaged upstream `codex` runtime plugin.
- The Nix Codex launcher runs only for local stdio app-server launches selected by nix-openclaw. It reads OpenClaw-provided `CODEX_HOME`, sets `HOME=$CODEX_HOME/home`, links `$HOME/.nix-profile/bin` to the Nix-built runtime `bin` directory, prepends that bin dir, then execs upstream Codex.
- Home Manager activation no longer predicts Codex agent directories or creates `$CODEX_HOME/home/.nix-profile/bin`.

The Nix launcher is not offered when the user provides `plugins.entries.codex.config.appServer.command`, a configured or inherited `OPENCLAW_CODEX_APP_SERVER_BIN`, or `appServer.transport = "websocket"`.

`plugins.entries.codex.config.appServer.args` and `OPENCLAW_CODEX_APP_SERVER_ARGS` are different: upstream resolves args separately from the executable, so args still flow to the Nix launcher and then to upstream Codex. The current smoke check proves that distinction explicitly.

## HOME Compatibility

Upstream OpenClaw normally preserves inherited `HOME` for local Codex app-server launches. This Nix-managed launcher intentionally narrows `HOME` to `$CODEX_HOME/home` because Codex `command/exec` rebuilds command PATH around `$HOME/.nix-profile/bin`.

The smoke test includes the rejected alternative: before the Nix launcher, `$CODEX_HOME/home/.nix-profile/bin` is already linked to the Nix runtime `bin` directory, but `HOME` is still inherited and Codex command execution cannot resolve `gog`. That proves simply creating or prepending the `CODEX_HOME` profile in the parent launch environment is not enough for the actual Codex command path.

## Non-Goals

This PR does not add a public knob, does not add a host PATH fallback list, does not generate `OPENCLAW_CODEX_APP_SERVER_ARGS`, and does not enable the Codex plugin from `runtimePackages` alone.

## Upstream Contract Pointers

The code comments point future maintainers at the upstream contracts that matter:

- `docs/tools/exec.md`, `src/agents/agent-tools.ts`, and `src/agents/bash-tools.exec-runtime.ts` for OpenClaw command PATH handling and user path precedence.
- `extensions/codex/src/app-server/auth-bridge.ts`, `extensions/codex/src/app-server/config.ts`, and `docs/plugins/codex-harness-reference.md` for Codex `CODEX_HOME`, app-server command/args precedence, stdio vs WebSocket transport, inherited `HOME`, and env overrides.

## User Problem

OpenClaw-managed Codex command execution could not see Nix runtime tools such as `gog`.

That is narrower than “OpenClaw cannot run tools.” Generic OpenClaw command execution and Codex `command/exec` through the app-server are different paths. Upstream OpenClaw gives the Codex app-server a per-agent `CODEX_HOME`, but Codex command execution rebuilds command PATH around the app-server process `HOME`. In nix-openclaw, `runtimePackages` could be available to OpenClaw’s gateway/runtime tool path while Codex `command/exec` still failed with `gog: command not found`.

## Fix

This PR separates the two concepts and names the boundary in code and docs:

- `runtime-tools.nix` owns generic OpenClaw command tools: gateway `PATH` and generated upstream `tools.exec.pathPrepend`.
- Existing user `tools.exec.pathPrepend` entries stay ahead of generated `runtimePackages` entries, so Nix runtime packages fill in missing tools without shadowing an operator’s explicit command path.
- `codex-app-server.nix` owns only the Nix adapter for the packaged upstream `codex` runtime plugin.
- The Nix Codex launcher runs only for local stdio app-server launches selected by nix-openclaw. It reads OpenClaw-provided `CODEX_HOME`, sets `HOME=$CODEX_HOME/home`, links `$HOME/.nix-profile/bin` to the Nix-built runtime `bin` directory, prepends that bin dir, then execs upstream Codex.
- Home Manager activation no longer predicts Codex agent directories or creates `$CODEX_HOME/home/.nix-profile/bin`.

The Nix launcher is not offered when the user provides `plugins.entries.codex.config.appServer.command`, a configured or inherited `OPENCLAW_CODEX_APP_SERVER_BIN`, or `appServer.transport = "websocket"`.

`plugins.entries.codex.config.appServer.args` and `OPENCLAW_CODEX_APP_SERVER_ARGS` are different: upstream resolves args separately from the executable, so args still flow to the Nix launcher and then to upstream Codex. The current smoke check proves that distinction explicitly.

## HOME Compatibility

Upstream OpenClaw normally preserves inherited `HOME` for local Codex app-server launches. This Nix-managed launcher intentionally narrows `HOME` to `$CODEX_HOME/home` because Codex `command/exec` rebuilds command PATH around `$HOME/.nix-profile/bin`.

OpenClaw-managed Codex command execution could not see Nix runtime tools such as `gog`.

That is narrower than “OpenClaw cannot run tools.” Generic OpenClaw command execution and Codex `command/exec` through the app-server are different paths. Upstream OpenClaw gives the Codex app-server a per-agent `CODEX_HOME`, but Codex command execution rebuilds command PATH around the app-server process `HOME`. In nix-openclaw, `runtimePackages` could be available to OpenClaw’s gateway/runtime tool path while Codex `command/exec` still failed with `gog: command not found`.

## Fix

This PR separates the two concepts and names the boundary in code and docs:

- `runtime-tools.nix` owns generic OpenClaw command tools: gateway `PATH` and generated upstream `tools.exec.pathPrepend`.
- Existing user `tools.exec.pathPrepend` entries stay ahead of generated `runtimePackages` entries, so Nix runtime packages fill in missing tools without shadowing an operator’s explicit command path.
- `codex-app-server.nix` owns only the Nix adapter for the packaged upstream `codex` runtime plugin.
- The Nix Codex launcher runs only for local stdio app-server launches selected by nix-openclaw. It reads OpenClaw-provided `CODEX_HOME`, sets `HOME=$CODEX_HOME/home`, links `$HOME/.nix-profile/bin` to the Nix-built runtime `bin` directory, prepends that bin dir, then execs upstream Codex.
- Home Manager activation no longer predicts Codex agent directories or creates `$CODEX_HOME/home/.nix-profile/bin`.

The Nix launcher is not offered when the user provides `plugins.entries.codex.config.appServer.command`, a configured or inherited `OPENCLAW_CODEX_APP_SERVER_BIN`, or `appServer.transport = "websocket"`.

`plugins.entries.codex.config.appServer.args` and `OPENCLAW_CODEX_APP_SERVER_ARGS` are different: upstream resolves args separately from the executable, so args still flow to the Nix launcher and then to upstream Codex. The current smoke check proves that distinction explicitly.

## HOME Compatibility

Upstream OpenClaw normally preserves inherited `HOME` for local Codex app-server launches. This Nix-managed launcher intentionally narrows `HOME` to `$CODEX_HOME/home` because Codex `command/exec` rebuilds command PATH around `$HOME/.nix-profile/bin`.

The smoke test includes the rejected alternative: before the Nix launcher, `$CODEX_HOME/home/.nix-profile/bin` is already linked to the Nix runtime `bin` directory, but `HOME` is still inherited and Codex command execution cannot resolve `gog`. That proves simply creating or prepending the `CODEX_HOME` profile in the parent launch environment is not enough for the actual Codex command path.

## Non-Goals

This PR does not add a public knob, does not add a host PATH fallback list, does not generate `OPENCLAW_CODEX_APP_SERVER_ARGS`, and does not enable the Codex plugin from `runtimePackages` alone.

The code comments point future maintainers at the upstream contracts that matter:

- `docs/tools/exec.md`, `src/agents/agent-tools.ts`, and `src/agents/bash-tools.exec-runtime.ts` for OpenClaw command PATH handling and user path precedence.
- `extensions/codex/src/app-server/auth-bridge.ts`, `extensions/codex/src/app-server/config.ts`, and `docs/plugins/codex-harness-reference.md` for Codex `CODEX_HOME`, app-server command/args precedence, stdio vs WebSocket transport, inherited `HOME`, and env overrides.
